abstract: This entry refers to a 2025 work titled 'The State of Social Media Research APIs & Tools in the Digital Service Act Era.' No abstract or body text was provided, so the substantive content of the paper cannot be summarized here. The title indicates a focus on the availability and condition of social media research infrastructure in the context of the European Union's Digital Services Act (DSA).
framing: Based on the title alone, the paper appears to engage with debates in platform studies, internet governance, and computational social science about researcher access to social media data, situated within the regulatory context of the EU Digital Services Act.
